launcher.release_notes:
Feature/colima support (#106)

* cx-labs-launcher: detect Colima alongside Docker Desktop as container runtime

Docker Desktop requires a paid Business subscription for company use (orgs
> 250 seats or > $10M revenue, per Docker's terms). This adds first-class
support for Colima — free, MIT-licensed, brew-installable — as an
alternative container runtime for hosting the kind cluster.

Both runtimes expose the same Docker socket protocol, so kind, `make up`,
and every scenario deploy work unchanged. The launcher just gets smarter
about which runtime it's talking to.

Non-breaking for existing users: anyone already on Docker Desktop keeps
working exactly as before. When both are installed and both are running,
the launcher prefers Colima (cheaper).

k8s.rs
------
* New ContainerRuntime enum (Colima / DockerDesktop / None) and
  ContainerRuntimeStatus struct. DockerStatus kept as a type alias so
  the existing `get_docker_status` Tauri command and its TS shape
  stay wire-compatible.
* container_runtime_status() replaces the old docker-only probe with a
  five-step detection: (1) `colima status` running → Colima, (2) `docker
  info` succeeding → whichever runtime's socket is up, (3) Colima
  installed-but-stopped, (4) Docker CLI present but daemon dead,
  (5) nothing.
* install_local_cluster's runtime step auto-`colima start`s when Colima
  is installed but stopped — no user interaction. Docker Desktop still
  requires a manual .app launch (the launcher can't reliably start a
  GUI app from a subprocess).
* Colima start defaults: 2 CPU / 4 GB / 20 GB disk — sized for kind +
  scenario workloads with tiny-telemetry + otel-demo running.
* `docker` CLI added to REQUIRED_TOOLS. Docker Desktop bundles it;
  Colima users need `brew install docker` separately (colima's brew
  formula does not include a CLI). `colima` itself is deliberately NOT
  required, so Docker-Desktop-only users aren't nagged to install it.

Frontend
--------
* Top-bar pill now says "Colima running" / "Docker Desktop running" /
  "No container runtime installed" based on the runtime `kind`.
* DockerStatus TS shape gained an optional `kind` field mapping onto
  the Rust enum (older builds without it degrade to "container runtime"
  as label).

Scope
-----
* No scenario changes.
* No CI changes (kind + make up work identically on either runtime).
* No DoD / verify CLI changes.

Follow-up (Phase 2, separate PR):
  - README section explaining the license trade-off.
  - Optional in-app banner suggesting Colima migration to Docker-
    Desktop-only users.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* cx-labs-launcher: pill warning badge + Settings → Container runtime card

Follow-up to the Colima detection commit. When Docker Desktop is the
detected container runtime, the top-bar pill grows a small ⚠ badge with
a hover tooltip explaining the license situation. Clicking the badge
jumps to Settings and highlights the new "Container runtime" card, where
the user can one-click install Colima.

UI
--
* Top-bar pill: yellow ⚠ appended when kind == "docker_desktop"; hover
  reveals the license explanation. Clicking the badge activates the
  Settings nav and scrolls to the runtime card with a brief
  yellow-glow animation so it's obvious what changed.
* Settings gains a new "Container runtime" card showing the detected
  runtime + a license-warning box (only rendered when Docker Desktop is
  active) + an "Install Colima" primary button. Same card also serves
  users on Colima or with no runtime — the CTA + warning render
  conditionally.

Rust
----
* New `install_colima` Tauri command → `k8s::install_colima()`.
  `brew install colima docker && colima start`. Idempotent; safe to
  invoke on machines that already have parts of it.

Nothing about the existing Docker Desktop flow changes — users who
never open Settings and never click the ⚠ keep working exactly as
before.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* cx-labs-launcher: per-cluster runtime badge + runtime picker on install

Follow-up to Colima support. Without this the launcher was blind to WHICH
runtime physically hosts the kind cluster, so install/remove could target
the wrong docker socket when both Colima and Docker Desktop were up. This
makes the runtime dimension visible to the user and honored by every code
path that touches the cluster's container.

Behavioural changes
-------------------
* Install-local-cluster modal now shows a "Container runtime" picker
  when BOTH Colima and Docker Desktop are running. Options: Colima
  (default, free) and Docker Desktop (paid subscription for company
  use). Picker is hidden when only one runtime is up — the install
  just uses the available one.
* install_local_cluster now honors the picked runtime by resolving its
  docker unix socket via `docker context inspect <ctx>` and setting
  DOCKER_HOST on the `make up` subprocess. `kind` inside make up
  targets that specific runtime instead of the user's default context.
* delete_kind_cluster (Remove local cluster) auto-detects which runtime
  hosts the cluster via `docker --context=X ps` and sets DOCKER_HOST
  for `kind delete cluster` accordingly. Previously the launcher's
  default context won — if it was pointing at Colima but the cluster
  lived on Docker Desktop, delete said "cluster not found" without
  removing anything.
* K8s targets card in Settings shows a 🐋 Colima / 🐳 Docker Desktop
  badge next to the launcher-managed cluster name. External clusters
  have no badge.

Rust
----
* New AvailableRuntimes struct + `available_runtimes()` command driving
  the picker's visibility in the modal.
* New find_kind_cluster_runtime() probes both docker contexts for the
  cluster's control-plane container to attribute an existing cluster to
  its hosting runtime.
* New docker_socket_for(runtime) resolves the unix socket path via
  `docker context inspect`, robust across Colima / Docker Desktop
  version drift instead of hardcoding paths.
* ClusterInfo gains an optional `runtime: ContainerRuntime` field
  populated for local clusters only.
* install_local_cluster signature gains a `requested_runtime` param.
  install_local_cluster Tauri command accepts a matching optional
  `runtime` string ("colima" | "docker_desktop") from the modal.

Frontend
--------
* TypeScript ClusterInfo type mirrors the new optional runtime field.
* Runtime picker in install modal is populated on-open from
  available_runtimes(); default is Colima when both are up.
* K8s targets rows render a small runtime chip; row still has the
  existing "Make active" button for switching (user request; button
  was already there but easier to find now).

Zero-change surfaces
--------------------
* Deploy / verify / kubectl paths — unchanged. kubectl uses contexts,
  which are runtime-independent.
* Docker-Desktop-only users — unchanged. Picker hidden, no Colima nag
  during install unless they explicitly want it.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* cx-labs-launcher: trust hashicorp/tap before make up (Homebrew 5.0+ change)

Homebrew 5.0 (late 2025) added a "trust" requirement for third-party taps:
automated formula operations refuse to load formulas from untrusted taps.
An interactive `brew install hashicorp/tap/terraform` succeeds because
it prompts the user; the Ansible `homebrew` task inside cx-labs' `make
up` does not, and fails with:

    Error: Refusing to load formula hashicorp/tap/terraform from
    untrusted tap hashicorp/tap.
    Run `brew trust --formula hashicorp/tap/terraform` or
    `brew trust hashicorp/tap` to trust it.

The task fires even when terraform is already installed — ansible re-
checks on every run — so it blocks the whole cluster install on every
Mac running Homebrew ≥ 5.0.

Fix: run `brew tap hashicorp/tap && brew trust hashicorp/tap` from the
launcher before invoking `make up`, and also from the "install missing
prereqs" flow whenever terraform is in the missing list. Both commands
are idempotent — tap on already-added returns success, trust on
already-trusted returns success, and `brew trust` is a no-op on older
Homebrew (Unknown command → we log and continue instead of failing).

Step is never gated (ok:true even on non-zero) — the real check happens
inside make up and produces a much clearer error if trust actually
matters and somehow didn't take.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* cx-labs-launcher: 🦙 for Colima badge + Switch-runtime button on K8s targets

Two follow-ups on user feedback:

1. Colima was showing the whale emoji (🐋) but the whale is Docker's
   mascot (Moby). Colima's actual project mascot is a llama — the "C"
   comes from "Container Runtimes on Lima". Switched to 🦙 so the
   badges are visually distinct at a glance.

2. Added a "⇄ Switch to <other runtime>" button next to Remove on the
   K8s targets row when the cluster is on one runtime and the other is
   available. Only one cluster can exist at a time (kubectl context
   name is shared, kind stores metadata by container name), so
   switching means destroy + recreate — the button chains the existing
   remove_kind_cluster path with an install-modal re-open pre-selected
   at the target runtime. A confirm() warns that deployed scenarios
   will be lost.

The confirm() is the plain browser one for now — good enough for a
destructive action that surfaces the consequence clearly. Can promote
to a proper modal later if the UX warrants it.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* cx-labs-launcher: bump to 0.10.0 — coexisting Colima + Docker Desktop clusters

Colima- and Docker-Desktop-hosted clusters can now exist simultaneously
under distinct kubectl contexts. Switching between them is just clicking
"Make active" on the desired row in Settings → K8s targets — no
destroy-and-recreate involved. Each cluster keeps its own deployed
scenarios; switching preserves both sides' state.

Naming
------
Runtime-specific cluster + context + kubeconfig names — the cx-labs
Makefile already respects `KIND_CLUSTER_NAME` and `KUBECONFIG` env vars
(both `?=` defaults), so no cx-labs patching needed.

  Colima cluster:  cx-labs-kind-colima
  Colima context:  kind-cx-labs-kind-colima
  Colima kubeconf: ~/.kube/kind-cx-labs-colima.yaml

  Docker cluster:  cx-labs-kind-docker
  Docker context:  kind-cx-labs-kind-docker
  Docker kubeconf: ~/.kube/kind-cx-labs-docker.yaml

Legacy `cx-labs-kind-local` / `kind-cx-labs-kind-local` is still
recognized so users upgrading from 0.9.x see their existing cluster
without needing to reinstall. On next install the new names apply.

Rust
----
* New helpers in k8s.rs: `managed_local_cluster_name(runtime)`,
  `managed_local_context_name(runtime)`, `managed_local_kubeconfig_path
  (runtime)`, `is_managed_local_context(name)`, `runtime_from_context
  (name)`.
* install_local_cluster now returns `InstallLocalClusterResult { steps,
  resolved_runtime }` so the Tauri command can name the active-target
  context correctly after install.
* install_local_cluster passes KIND_CLUSTER_NAME + KUBECONFIG env vars
  into `make up` — each runtime lands in its own kubeconfig file, so
  merged_kubeconfig() picks up both as distinct contexts.
* Every previous reference to the removed `LOCAL_CLUSTER_NAME` /
  `LOCAL_CONTEXT_NAME` constants now goes through
  `is_managed_local_context()` (prefix match on `kind-cx-labs-kind-*`)
  or the runtime-specific helpers.
* prettify() still says "Local (cx-labs kind)" for both runtime-
  suffixed contexts — the runtime badge in the row disambiguates them
  visually.

Frontend
--------
* Removed the "⇄ Switch to X" destroy-and-recreate button — no longer
  needed. Users switch active target by clicking "Make active" on the
  other runtime's row (button was already there).
* Everything else on the K8s-targets card unchanged — runtime badges
  still render, Remove still respects the per-cluster DOCKER_HOST
  pinning added in the previous commit.

Terraform state (known caveat)
------------------------------
The cx-labs `make apply` (used only for the cluster-generator toggles)
shares terraform state across both clusters — the toggle applied to
one cluster overwrites the other's terraform view. The launcher only
runs `make apply` at install time today, so this is latent; if we ever
expose per-cluster toggles as a runtime action, we'll need per-cluster
tf workspaces.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* cx-labs-launcher: restore shadow kubeconfig when deleting a legacy-named cluster

Bug: when the user had two clusters with the same internal name on
different sockets (typically a legacy "cx-labs-kind-local" on both
Colima and Docker Desktop from before the v0.10 per-runtime naming),
removing one of them ripped the kubectl context out of every
~/.kube/*.yaml file — including the one pointing at the surviving
cluster on the other socket. That cluster kept running as a Docker
container but disappeared from the launcher's K8s targets list.

Fix: before the kubeconfig purge, scan the OTHER runtime's socket for
a same-named cluster ("shadow"). After the purge, `kind export
kubeconfig` against that socket to reinstate the shadow cluster's
kubectl context.

Only triggers for legacy shared-name clusters — new v0.10+ installs
use runtime-suffixed names (`cx-labs-kind-colima` / -docker) so the
purge only touches the intended entry and the shadow-restore is a
no-op.

Recovery for anyone who already hit this pre-fix, one command from a
terminal (surviving cluster's container must still be running):

    DOCKER_CONTEXT=default kind export kubeconfig --name cx-labs-kind-local

Restart the launcher; K8s targets shows the restored cluster.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* cx-labs-launcher: pass TF_VAR_kubeconfig_context/path into make up

Terraform's kubernetes + helm providers in cx-labs/kind-local/tf/provider.tf
read `var.kubeconfig_context` and `var.kubeconfig_path`, NOT the KUBECONFIG
environment variable. The defaults in variables.tf are the legacy
"kind-cx-labs-kind-local" / "~/.kube/kind-local.yaml".

Since v0.10 install creates a runtime-suffixed context
(kind-cx-labs-kind-colima or -docker) via KIND_CLUSTER_NAME, the terraform
apply step failed with:

    Error: Provider configuration: cannot load Kubernetes client config
    context "kind-cx-labs-kind-local" does not exist

Fix: pass the runtime-specific values through as TF_VAR_kubeconfig_context
and TF_VAR_kubeconfig_path env vars on the make invocation. Terraform picks
them up automatically (TF_VAR_ convention), no cx-labs patching required.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* cx-labs-launcher: append runtime to cluster display name (target picker disambig)

Two "Local (cx-labs kind)" entries in the top-bar target picker were
indistinguishable when both a Colima- and Docker-Desktop-hosted cluster
existed. The Settings K8s targets card had a colored chip badge that made
the distinction clear, but the target picker's <select> only renders text
options — no room for chips.

Fix: bake the runtime into the display_name string on the Rust side:

  Local (cx-labs kind) · 🦙 Colima
  Local (cx-labs kind) · 🐳 Docker Desktop

The Settings K8s targets card would double up (suffix + chip badge), so
the frontend strips the runtime suffix when rendering that row and keeps
the chip. Top-bar picker shows the full label. Legacy clusters (from
before v0.10 per-runtime naming) whose runtime we can't infer fall back
to just "Local (cx-labs kind)".

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* cx-labs-launcher: pill + Container Runtime card show BOTH runtimes independently

Previous behaviour picked a single "winning" runtime in the pill and the
Container Runtime card, which was misleading when both Colima and Docker
Desktop were running side-by-side. Rewrite so per-runtime status is
always surfaced, and codify the policy that Colima is the only runtime
the launcher installs — Docker Desktop is bring-your-own.

Behaviour
---------
* Top-bar pill lists every running runtime:
    "🦙 Colima + 🐳 Docker Desktop running"   both up
    "🦙 Colima running"                        Colima only
    "🐳 Docker Desktop running"                Docker Desktop only
    "🦙 Colima — none running"                 installed, stopped
    "No container runtime"                     nothing to work with

* Container Runtime card in Settings shows two lines, one per runtime,
  each with its own state (running / installed but not running / not
  installed). Docker Desktop's "not installed" state reads
  "not installed — bring your own" to communicate the license policy
  without another paragraph of copy.

* Install-Colima button is shown only when Colima isn't installed —
  never for Docker Desktop (paid subscription, not our place to install).

* Install-cluster modal's runtime picker uses the per-runtime running
  flags (both must be running to show the choice).

Rust
----
* AvailableRuntimes gained {colima_installed, colima_running,
  docker_desktop_installed, docker_desktop_running}. Docker Desktop
  installed = /Applications/Docker.app exists (checking `docker` on
  PATH is unreliable because Colima's brew formula also installs the CLI).
* Every caller (`install_local_cluster`, install modal, pill) updated.

Frontend
--------
* refreshDockerStatus fetches available_runtimes directly instead of
  get_docker_status (which returned a single "winning" runtime).
* renderRuntimeSection builds a two-line status block from the same
  data source, with per-runtime coloured state text.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* cx-labs-launcher: re-probe deployed scenarios when active target changes

deployedRows was a session-local Set populated only by this window's
Run/Remove clicks — no connection to actual cluster state. Deploy on
Docker, switch to Colima, and scenario 001 still shows Check/Remove
even though it lives on the OTHER cluster.

Add `scenario_deployed_on(context, manifests_dir)` — runs
`kubectl get -f <dir> -o name --ignore-not-found` and returns whether
any of the scenario's specific named resources exist on that context.
Exit-code guarded so a context-lookup failure isn't misread as "not
deployed" (which would silently hide real deployments).

Expose as `list_deployed_scenarios(context) -> Vec<u32>` — fans out
one probe per scenario in parallel (20+ scenarios sequentially would
be visibly slow on target switch).

Frontend calls the new command from three sites: refreshClusters
(initial + reload), the top-bar target-picker change handler, and
the Settings "Make active" button. Optimistic add/remove on
deploy_scenario/remove_scenario is kept — target switch is what
snaps state back to ground truth.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
